title: ononis campestris var. antiquorum
intro: Ononis campestris var. antiquorum, commonly known as the Field Woodruff or Field Burnet, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Ononis campestris var. antiquorum include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as its use in treating respiratory problems like coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be dried and used as a herbal supplement or added to soups and stews for its medicinal properties.
